refnamediv.refpurpose: Run a command with a custom terminal background color or title

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: systemd-pty-forward can be used to run a command with a custom terminal background color or title.

listitem.para: Change the terminal background color to the specified ANSI color as long as the command runs. The color specified should be an ANSI X3.64 SGR background color, i.e. strings such as 40, 41, …, 47, 48;2;…, 48;5;…. See ANSI Escape Code (Wikipedia) for details.
Change the terminal title to the specified string as long as the command runs.
Suppresses additional informational output while running.
Selects how the terminal for the invoked command is acquired and connected. Takes one of interactive, read-only, broker or broker-log.

If interactive (the default) a pseudo TTY is allocated locally and the calling terminal is forwarded to it interactively, i.e. both terminal output and user input are forwarded. If read-only this is similar, but no user input from standard input is forwarded to the command.

If broker or broker-log the pseudo TTY is not allocated locally, but acquired from systemd-ptybrokerd.service8. In both cases the broker retains the frontend (i.e. the "master") side of the pseudo TTY and the command is invoked on the acquired backend (i.e. the "slave") side. With broker the frontend output is discarded (unless monitored), with broker-log it is written to the logs by systemd-ptybrokerd.service. The allocated pseudo TTY may subsequently be monitored, listed or hung up with ptyctl1.

When --console=broker or --console=broker-log is used, selects whether to talk to the system-wide instance or the per-user instance of systemd-ptybrokerd.service8. Defaults to --system. Has no effect in the other console modes.

When --console=broker or --console=broker-log is used, registers the newly allocated pseudo TTY under the specified name with systemd-ptybrokerd.service8; if not specified (the default) the broker picks a randomized name. Has no effect in the other console modes.
